failure_modes.text: Maslow's-hammer style framework use can optimize the wrong delivery problem because the team mistakes a familiar pipeline story for the real system.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge whether the familiar pipeline story fits the current delivery problem; Maslow's hammer can optimize the wrong bottleneck.

failure_modes.text: Boiling-the-ocean analysis can delay the feedback loop that should have revealed the next operational constraint earlier.
failure_modes.mitigation: Ship the smallest feedback loop that reveals the next operational constraint; boiling-the-ocean analysis delays the signal the pipeline should provide.

failure_modes.text: Local pipeline optimization can quietly increase rollback pain, debugging latency, or production brittleness.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when local pipeline optimization hides system fragility
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit local pipeline optimizations for downstream side effects: faster deploys that increase rollback pain or debugging latency are not net improvements.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: Which output can we measure now to detect whether faster local flow is actually degrading end-to-end reliability?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What key output can I measure now to detect failure early?

heuristics.text: Use a build-observe-diagnose-adjust loop so learning happens continuously instead of after a large release batch.
heuristics.source_quote: shortening the distance between build, observe, diagnose, and adjust

heuristics.text: Map handoffs, merge friction, and brittle release steps explicitly before optimizing tool speed or throughput numbers.
heuristics.source_quote: handoffs, untested merges, delayed feedback, brittle release steps

heuristics.text: Use the five-step sequence of goals, problems, diagnosis, design, and implementation so delivery fixes do not skip root-cause work.
heuristics.source_quote: Success requires doing all five steps well, one at a time, and in order

curation_notes.summary: Kept the intervention layer on explicit operating loops, integration-friction diagnosis, and anti-local-optimization safeguards.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broad platform or tooling catalogs because the raw source is stronger on system-loop and reliability discipline.
open_questions: The third premortem question is reviewed compression from the source's early-failure-detection and worst-case logic.

allies: The source is rich on systems-thinking, design-thinking, and game-theory style simulation use.
antagonists: The raw source names margin-of-safety, optimization-theory, and confirmation-bias pressures clearly.
structured_tensions: The source explicitly preserves tensions against margin-of-safety, optimization-theory, and confirmation-bias.
